title: US Parasite Outbreak Reaches 2,800 Cases
subtitle: Health officials struggle to identify the source of a growing infection as funding cuts complicate investigation efforts.

summary.en: A parasitic infection called cyclosporiasis has now reached over 2,800 cases across the United States. The illness causes severe stomach problems, including watery diarrhea and weight loss. While the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention reports lower confirmed numbers, state officials in Michigan and Ohio are seeing a much larger impact. Experts suggest that recent federal funding cuts have made it harder for health departments to track the source of the outbreak. Investigators are currently urging the public to wash or cook produce thoroughly to avoid infection.
summary.zh: 一種稱為環孢子蟲症的寄生蟲感染在美國已累計超過 2,800 例。該疾病會引起嚴重胃部問題，包括水瀉與體重減輕。儘管美國疾病管制與預防中心通報的確診數較低，但密西根州與俄亥俄州的官員觀察到疫情影響更為巨大。專家認為，近期聯邦資金的削減使得衛生部門更難追蹤疫情源頭。調查人員目前呼籲大眾徹底清洗或烹煮農產品，以避免感染。

paragraphs.en: A growing outbreak of a parasitic infection known as cyclosporiasis has now reached more than 2,800 cases across the United States. This illness, which is caused by a tiny parasite, leads to symptoms such as watery diarrhea, a loss of appetite, and significant weight loss. While health authorities continue their work to identify the source of the sickness, the scale of the outbreak has raised serious questions about the ability of public health systems to respond effectively during a crisis.
paragraphs.zh: 一種稱為「環孢子蟲症」的寄生蟲感染疫情正持續擴大，目前全美病例已超過 2,800 例。這種由微小寄生蟲引起的疾病，會導致水瀉、食慾不振以及體重顯著下降等症狀。儘管衛生當局持續努力追查病源，但此次疫情的規模已引發大眾對公共衛生系統在危機期間應對能力的嚴重質疑。

paragraphs.en: According to The Guardian Health, the situation is particularly serious in Michigan, where officials have reported 2,640 cases. Nearby, officials in Ohio have identified another 177 cases. These numbers are notably higher than the figures released by the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC). On Friday, the CDC reported 843 confirmed cases and 1,500 suspected cases across 31 states. The agency noted that 86 people have been hospitalized, though fortunately, no deaths have been linked to the outbreak so far. The CDC expects these numbers to rise, partly because of the natural delays that occur when investigating any disease.
paragraphs.zh: 根據《衛報健康版》（The Guardian Health）報導，密西根州的情況尤為嚴重，當地官員已通報 2,640 例病例。鄰近的俄亥俄州官員也確認了另外 177 例。這些數字顯著高於美國疾病管制與預防中心（CDC）所發布的數據。週五時，CDC 通報全美 31 州共有 843 例確診病例及 1,500 例疑似病例。該機構指出，目前已有 86 人住院治療，所幸截至目前為止尚未傳出死亡案例。CDC 預計病例數還會上升，部分原因是調查任何疾病時都會出現的自然延遲現象。

paragraphs.en: Investigating an outbreak like this is a difficult task. Cyclospora has a two-week incubation period, which is the time between catching the parasite and the first appearance of symptoms. Because of this, experts often have to wait several weeks before they can interview patients. By the time investigators ask people what they ate or where they shopped, many patients struggle to remember the details. Dr. Natasha Bagdasarian Natasha Bagdasarian, the chief medical executive in Michigan, told the Associated Press that despite these challenges, there is clearly a linked outbreak happening right now.
paragraphs.zh: 調查這類疫情是一項艱鉅的任務。環孢子蟲的潛伏期為兩週，也就是從感染寄生蟲到出現症狀的時間。正因如此，專家往往需要等待數週才能訪談病患。等到調查人員詢問民眾吃了什麼或在哪裡購物時，許多病患已難以回憶細節。密西根州首席醫療執行官娜塔莎·巴格達薩里安（Natasha Bagdasarian）向美聯社表示，儘管面臨這些挑戰，但目前顯然正發生一起關聯性的疫情。

paragraphs.en: Experts are concerned that the investigation is being slowed down by recent changes in government support. Barbara Kowalcyk, an associate professor at the George Washington University’s Milken Institute of Public Health, explained that tracking an outbreak is like solving a puzzle. She noted that when pieces of that puzzle are removed, it becomes much harder to see the whole picture. According to The Guardian Health, the Trump administration cut $11.4 billion in grants to state and local health departments in March 2025. These cuts have forced some departments to reduce staff or move employees to part-time roles, which limits their ability to scale up their work during an emergency.
paragraphs.zh: 專家擔憂，近期政府支援的變動正拖慢調查進度。喬治華盛頓大學米爾肯公共衛生學院（Milken Institute of Public Health）副教授芭芭拉·科瓦爾奇克（Barbara Kowalcyk）解釋，追蹤疫情就像解謎。她指出，當拼圖碎片被移除時，要看清全貌就變得困難許多。根據《衛報健康版》報導，川普政府在 2025 年 3 月削減了 114 億美元對州及地方衛生部門的補助。這些刪減迫使部分部門裁減人力或將員工轉為兼職，限制了他們在緊急情況下擴大作業的能力。

paragraphs.en: Kowalcyk stated that the funding cuts have likely made the typical delays in disease reporting even worse. She explained that if a department is understaffed, they might not be able to interview patients until six or eight weeks after they become ill. This makes the job of epidemiologists—scientists who study how diseases spread—much harder. Furthermore, the administration reduced the remit, or the range of responsibilities, of a program specifically designed to coordinate information on foodborne illnesses. This reduction in support has made it more difficult for officials to connect the dots between cases in different states.
paragraphs.zh: 科瓦爾奇克表示，資金削減很可能使疾病通報中常見的延遲情況更加惡化。她解釋，如果部門人力不足，他們可能無法在病患發病後的六到八週內進行訪談。這使得流行病學家（研究疾病傳播方式的科學家）的工作變得更加困難。此外，政府還縮減了一個專門用於協調食源性疾病資訊計畫的職權範圍。這種支援的減少，使得官員更難以將不同州之間的病例連結起來。

paragraphs.en: While investigators work to find the origin of the parasite, health departments are taking steps to protect the public. In Michigan, officials are urging restaurants and commercial kitchens in the south-east part of the state to be extra careful. They recommend that all leafy greens, snow peas, certain herbs, and raspberries should be washed thoroughly. Ideally, these items should be cooked before they are served to ensure that any parasites are killed.
paragraphs.zh: 在調查人員努力尋找寄生蟲來源的同時，衛生部門也採取措施保護大眾。在密西根州，官員呼籲該州東南部的餐廳與商業廚房務必格外謹慎。他們建議所有葉菜類、荷蘭豆、特定香草及覆盆子都應徹底清洗。理想情況下，這些食材在供應前應先烹煮，以確保寄生蟲被消滅。

paragraphs.en: As the investigation continues, the focus remains on finding the common link between the thousands of people who have become sick. Whether it is a specific food item or a particular store, finding the source is the only way to stop the spread. However, as Kowalcyk observed, the current lack of resources means that the process is moving slower than it would have in the past. For now, the public is advised to follow local health guidelines and practice good food safety habits while waiting for more information from federal and state authorities.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著調查持續進行，焦點仍放在找出數千名病患之間的共同連結。無論是特定食品還是特定商店，找出源頭是阻止疫情擴散的唯一途徑。然而，正如科瓦爾奇克所觀察到的，目前資源匱乏意味著調查進度比過去緩慢。目前，大眾被建議遵循當地衛生準則，並在等待聯邦與州政府進一步資訊的同時，保持良好的食品安全習慣。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To increase the size, amount, or importance of something.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見於商業或組織運作，指提升處理能力。文中：These cuts have forced some departments to reduce staff or move employees to part-time roles, which limits their ability to scale up their work during an emergency.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To understand the relationship between different facts or pieces of information.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 將零散的資訊連結起來，找出關聯性。
keyTerms.noteZh: 字面上是連連看，引申為從線索中推導出真相。文中：This reduction in support has made it more difficult for officials to connect the dots between cases in different states.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Connected or related to something else.
keyTerms.noteZh: 這裡形容疫情爆發並非偶然，而是有共同關聯。文中：Dr. Natasha Bagdasarian Natasha Bagdasarian, the chief medical executive in Michigan, told the Associated Press that despite these challenges, there is clearly a linked outbreak happening right now.

sources.name: The Guardian Health
sources.title: US outbreak of parasite causing ‘watery diarrhea’ rises to more than 2,800 cases

previousArticles.title: Health Officials Work to Find Source of Growing Parasite Outbreak
previousArticles.summaryEn: A parasitic illness called cyclosporiasis is spreading across 31 states in the U.S. Health authorities report hundreds of confirmed cases, though the actual number is likely much higher. The illness causes stomach issues such as diarrhea and nausea. Investigators are working to identify the specific food source behind the current surge. Officials advise the public to wash produce thoroughly and follow food safety rules.

previousArticles.title: Health Officials Track Growing Parasite Outbreak Across the United States
previousArticles.summaryEn: A parasitic illness called cyclosporiasis is spreading across 31 U.S. states. The infection causes stomach problems like diarrhea and nausea. Health officials have reported hundreds of cases, but the true number is likely much higher. Investigators are still working to find the specific food or water source responsible. Experts advise people to wash produce thoroughly and follow food safety rules.

previousArticles.title: Ebola Outbreak in Congo Spreads to New Areas as Death Toll Rises
previousArticles.summaryEn: The Democratic Republic of the Congo is facing its fastest-growing Ebola outbreak in history. Official data shows 1,759 confirmed cases and 600 deaths across several provinces. The virus has also spread to neighboring Uganda, causing further concern for health officials. Ongoing armed conflicts and a lack of international funding are making it difficult to control the disease. Researchers have recently begun clinical trials to test potential treatments for this rare virus.

quiz.question: What is the primary reason why investigators struggle to obtain accurate information from patients during the current cyclosporiasis outbreak?

options.A: Patients are unwilling to cooperate with health officials due to privacy concerns.
options.B: The two-week incubation period causes patients to forget details about their recent diet and shopping habits.
options.C: Government budget cuts have eliminated the staff members responsible for conducting patient interviews.
options.D: The symptoms of the illness are so severe that patients are often unable to communicate with investigators.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第三段，Cyclospora 有兩週的潛伏期，這導致調查人員必須等待數週才能訪談患者，而患者往往已經難以回憶起當時吃了什麼或在哪裡購物。選項 A 文中未提及；選項 C 雖然提到預算削減導致人力不足，但不是造成「患者難以回憶」的主要原因；選項 D 雖然提到症狀，但並未說明症狀嚴重到無法溝通。

quiz.question: Based on the information provided, what can be inferred about the impact of the 2025 government funding cuts on public health?

options.A: They have directly caused the cyclosporiasis outbreak to spread to more states than originally anticipated.
options.B: They have made it impossible for health departments to provide any guidance on food safety to the public.
options.C: They have exacerbated existing investigative delays, making it harder to track the source of the disease.
options.D: They have resulted in the complete closure of all state and local health departments across the country.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章第四段與第五段指出，政府削減預算導致人手不足與協調能力下降，這使得調查人員難以連結不同地區的病例，並加劇了疾病報告的延遲。選項 A 過度推論，文中未說預算削減是導致擴散的原因；選項 B 錯誤，因為文中提到衛生部門仍有提供食物安全建議；選項 D 誇大其詞，文中僅提到裁員或改為兼職，而非完全關閉。

quiz.question: In the fifth paragraph, what does the word 'remit' most likely mean as used in the context of the government program?

options.A: The total amount of funding allocated to a specific project.
options.B: The official scope of responsibilities or authority assigned to an organization.
options.C: The time limit imposed on a research project to complete its findings.
options.D: The number of staff members permitted to work on a government initiative.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。文中提到「reduced the remit, or the range of responsibilities」，明確定義了 remit 在此處的意思為職責範圍。選項 A 指的是經費，文中已用 grants 或 funding 表達；選項 C 指的是期限；選項 D 指的是人力。這些選項皆不符合文中對該詞的直接解釋與上下文語境。

quiz.question: What is the central message of the article regarding the current cyclosporiasis outbreak?

options.A: The outbreak is primarily confined to Michigan and Ohio, requiring only local intervention.
options.B: Cyclosporiasis is a deadly disease that has overwhelmed the national healthcare system.
options.C: The outbreak highlights the critical need for better food safety regulations in commercial kitchens.
options.D: Resource limitations and administrative challenges are significantly hindering efforts to identify and control the outbreak.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 D。整篇文章的核心在於討論為何這次疫情調查困難，重點在於政府預算削減、人力不足以及調查程序的延遲，這些因素共同阻礙了疫情源頭的追查。選項 A 忽略了全國性的影響；選項 B 錯誤，文中提到目前無人死亡；選項 C 雖有提到食物安全，但這只是防疫措施的一部分，並非文章探討的整體核心問題。
